content.claim: International evidence on whether school consolidation produces per-pupil cost savings is contested: rural New York studies show 31.5-61.7% net savings after adjustment costs, but multi-state U.S. research found no statistically significant relationship between enrollment and per-pupil cost, and a Texas study of 20 consolidated districts found no post-consolidation savings.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Evidence base is non-Hungarian; Hungarian cost structure may differ
Studies span different time periods and methodologies
Capital/adjustment cost treatment varies across studies
Transportation costs often incompletely accounted for in savings estimates
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
